Important message from the President’s Office


Dear colleagues and students,



In light of the latest development of the Novel Coronavirus (2019-nCoV) outbreak, classes at the University will be suspended after the Chinese New Year holidays and will resume Monday, February 17, 2020. Detailed arrangements about in-class examinations, online teaching and make-up classes will be announced in due course.



Non-local students who are in Hong Kong are advised to stay in Hong Kong during the period when classes are suspended. Those who are not in Hong Kong at present should consider not returning to Hong Kong at this point in time. Research postgraduates should discuss their study plans with their supervisors.



We have strengthened preventive measures as far as practicable across the University. However, students and colleagues, especially those in residential halls, should take proper precautions related to personal hygiene and prevention of transmission or infection. Please consult the advice and announcements provided by the University Health Service at http://www.uhs.hku.hk/phpedpr/novel_infectious_disease_of_PH_significance.php

In order to safeguard the health of all University members, and to be able to provide appropriate assistance to affected individuals, all students and colleagues returning from Mainland China must complete an online health screening form, which will be emailed to University members.



The campus will remain open, and office and research labs will continue to operate, but heads of units and supervisors should communicate clearly with colleagues about working arrangements and exercise discretion regarding staffing flexibility as necessary, including work at home arrangements for some colleagues.



Events on campus in the coming weeks, especially events involving a large number of participants, should be rescheduled or cancelled.



The University has established a Task Force on Infectious Diseases, chaired by Vice-President (IA) Professor Norman Tien, to continually monitor and assess the situation. We will post updates to the University’s FaceBook page, HKU App and HKU Portal and website as appropriate and send bulk emails as necessary.



Please stay vigilant and continue to make your personal health a top priority.




Professor Xiang Zhang

President and Vice-Chancellor

面對疫情爆發，港共政權為粉飾太平，拒絕實施出入境管理措施，更執意就《禁止蒙面規例》上訴，將政治考慮淩駕於公眾安全，更任由醫護人員淪為政治工具，醫治大批中國患者。我等對此表示強烈譴責。

香港大學早前曾按衛生防護中心指引，安排懷疑感染武漢肺炎的同學送院。相關同學曾到訪明華綜合大樓及太古堂，校園安全未能保證。故此，我等要求校方：

一）立即停課直至武漢肺炎疫情受控
二）強制要求所有中國入境的學生進行健康申報
三）謝絕一切中國旅行團到訪校園
四）公開所有疫情控制措施及實行情況，以及所有於校內發現的受感染或有可能受感染之案例

我城人心惶惶之際，我校定當肩起守護學生的責任。危急存亡之秋，我等亦當與同學並肩作戰。

發起團體：香港大學學生會二零二零年度中央幹事會候選內閣

Joint Statement of HKU Students Regarding the Request of Suspension of Classes

(To sign the petition, please click on this link https://forms.gle/uDUQxRVVDDEqt2bT9 )

In face of the outbreak of Novel coronavirus (Wuhan Pneumonia) epidemic, the Hong Kong-Communist Regime refused to implement the immigration management measurement in the attempt to paper over the cracks. Moreover, they insist on appealing the Prohibition on Face Covering Regulation, putting political considerations above public safety. Medical staff are even forced to become a political tool to provide treatment for the huge amount of Chinese patient. We hereby express our vigorous denounce in response.

The University of Hong Kong has previously arranged the students who were suspected of being infected with Wuhan Pneumonia to the hospital in accordance with the guidelines of the Centre for Health Protection. The students in question visited Meng Wah Complex and Swire Hall. Such caused doubts among the students about the safety of the campus. Seeing as such, we would like to demand the school to:

1. Suspend all classes until the Wuhan Pneumonia epidemic is under control
2. Implement mandatory health declaration from all students crossing the border from China
3. Decline all Chinese Tour groups from entering and visiting the campus
4. Disclose all epidemic control measures and their application and any infected or suspected case on campus

During this time of crisis, the University shall shoulder the responsibility of protecting the students. We shall always stand by the students in this crucial time.

Organizer: Proposed Cabinet of Executive Committee, The Hong Kong University Students' Union, Session 2020

城大本科生在外遊時確診新型冠狀病毒



大學今日（2月20日）得悉，城大林姓本地本科生及其家人在鑽石公主號郵輪上，確診感染新型冠狀病毒，目前正在日本長野縣的醫院接受治療。郭位校長及學務長在收到消息後，即時致電該生，了解需要。該生感謝大學關心，希望香港政府可以加緊防疫工作，及照顧目前仍然滯留外地的港人。



郭位校長應林同學的請求，已向相關政府部門轉達有關訊息，呼籲政府莫延誤防疫工作，盡快跟進滯留外地的個案，主動提供適切支援。



大學關注並協調林同學的學習進度，希望他及其家人安心休養、早日康復。



傳訊及公關處啟
